one_liner: SPY at $751.71 is pressing the Wyckoff resistance at $756.39 with RSI 62.8 — Josh flagged that bulls need a close above $752 this week or the local lower-highs downtrend reasserts; CPI next week is the key macro catalyst.

price_action.vol_rank: Below average — moderate volume at key resistance level
price_action.intraday_story: SPY opened at $747.35, pressed steadily higher throughout the session to close at $751.71 near the daily high. Volume was below average. The close is essentially at Josh's identified key level of $752 — today's session is the make-or-break close the bulls needed.

news_catalyst.headline: SPY Presses $752 Resistance — CPI Next Week and Big Tech Earnings Are the Catalysts
news_catalyst.summary: US equity futures were mixed pre-bell amid renewed US-Iran tensions and SK Hynix's market debut. The S&P 500 pressed key resistance at $752 (Josh's level) with a close essentially at that number. CPI next week is the primary market mover. Delta Airlines earnings today serve as a consumer health check — any weakness would signal the market needs to cool before earnings season.

technical.summary: SPY is approaching the Wyckoff resistance at $756.39 with RSI at 62.8 — strong enough to push higher but not overbought. The close at $751.71 is essentially at Josh's $752 threshold. Above-EMA50 ($734.03) and EMA200 ($726.90) confirms the technical uptrend. The Gareth Soloway 9th-inning macro warning provides the longer-term bearish overlay — local technical setup is bullish, macro cycle is bearish.
technical.wyckoff_narrative: Neutral Wyckoff but pressing against range resistance at $756.39. A sustained close above $756 would be a Sign of Strength for the next leg. Failure here would form the lower high that Josh identified as the local downtrend pattern.

key_concerns: Gareth Soloway's cycle analysis warns of 9th-inning bull market with 75% downside risk over 1-2 years
fundamentals.summary: C grade as a macro instrument. Technically pressuring resistance with CPI and earnings as the next binary events.

risks: Failure to close above $752 by end of week creates a lower high in the local downtrend (Josh's framework)
CPI next week — hot print would reverse the current rally and pressure $734 EMA50 support
Gareth Soloway's macro cycle analysis: 9th inning of a bull market with risk of 75% drawdown over 1-2 years
